evals.prompt: When I draw a rectangle on the map, zoom in a couple of notches, and hit undo, the rectangle ends up in the wrong place — it seems to re-anchor itself somewhere else. Can you figure out what's going on and fix it?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Builds/consults the sequence matrix, identifies the undo × _lastSync hazard (undo restores x/y + _lastSync from a different camera era), writes a failing vitest repro at the factory level (buildGeoAnchorHandler with a mocked map whose zoom changed between edit and undo), then proposes a protocol-level fix (validate _lastSync against current camera) rather than a per-channel patch. Runs the full invariant suite after fixing.

evals.prompt: Audit the anchoring and zoom behavior of drawings for robustness — I don't have a specific bug, I just want to know what sequences of operations can corrupt an element's position or size.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers proactively (no named bug). Produces the operation-channel matrix, triages all seven known hazards (confirmed-and-fixed / deferred-with-issue / not-reproducible with proving test), prioritizes stock-Excalidraw channels (paste/undo/duplicate) that bypass the _lastSync protocol, and writes property tests for idempotency, round-trip epsilon, and zoom-in-then-out identity across all 3 scale modes × 3 anchor kinds.

evals.prompt: Pasted pins keep jumping to a slightly different spot the first time I pan the map after pasting. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Identifies hazard 1 (paste deep-clones geo AND stale _lastSync at a new offset, so the next onChange reads it as user-moved and spuriously re-anchors). Failing repro test first; fix at the protocol or clone-sanitization layer (strip/refresh _lastSync on paste path), not by making screen coords authoritative.

evals.prompt: The pin toolbar button looks misaligned when the map is zoomed — the icon inside the button is off-center. Fix the styling.
evals.expected_output: NEGATIVE: skill should NOT trigger despite the words 'pin', 'map', 'zoomed'. This is a CSS/UI concern — atlasdraw-ui-conventions is the right skill. No sequence matrix, no anchor tests.

evals.prompt: Refactor CoordinateSync and useGeoAnchor — they both have the same point/bbox/polyline switch, looks like copy-paste. Merge them into one shared reprojection function.
evals.expected_output: NEGATIVE-ISH: the canonicalization-verify-first rule governs (the two switches are deliberately separate — different direction and lifecycle; do not merge). If geo-op-idempotency-hunt loads via path triggers, it must reinforce the do-not-merge constraint, not perform the merge.
